task_completion.description: Open the raw OCR document and the correction guide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor (e.g., Calligra Words or LibreOffice) is open displaying 'lewis_clark_ocr_raw.odt', and a text editor is open displaying 'ocr_correction_guide.txt'.

task_completion.description: Remove arbitrary hard line breaks and rejoin hyphenated words.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The journal body text appears as continuous paragraphs without premature line breaks in the middle of sentences, and words previously split by hyphens across lines are rejoined into single words.

task_completion.description: Correct the specific OCR artifacts listed in the guide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text contains the correct words ('morning', 'government', 'against', '1804') instead of the original OCR errors ('moming', 'govemment', 'agarnst', 'l804'). This might be visible via Find/Replace dialogs or in the final text.

task_completion.description: Format all daily entry headers as Bold and Center Aligned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date headers (e.g., 'Monday, May 14, 1804') are visually centered on the page and have a bold font weight applied.

task_completion.description: Format all journal body text to be Justified with a 0.5 inch First Line Indent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The body paragraphs have straight, flush margins on both the left and right sides (Justified), and the first line of each paragraph is indented by approximately 0.5 inches (1.27 cm).
The agent clicks the 'Save' button, uses a save shortcut, or the document tab indicates there are no unsaved changes after formatting is complete.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor to edit the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with a graphical word processor (like Calligra Words) rather than using terminal commands (like sed or awk) to manipulate the .odt file.

integrity.description: The agent used proper formatting tools rather than manual spacing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses the alignment, bold, and indentation tools in the word processor's toolbar or paragraph settings, rather than manually inserting spaces/tabs to center text or indent lines.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to bypass manual editing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent performs the edits and formatting within the word processor interface, rather than running a Python or bash script in the terminal to generate the final document.
